name: Victor Thorne
recent_abstracts: Project Mycelium: Resilient Bio-Organic Computing via Stress-Induced Protein Self-Assembly. Synthetic proteins engineered with destabilized hydrophobic cores undergo accelerated self-assembly under thermal and osmotic stress, forming networked filament architectures with measurable electrical conductance exhibiting hysteresis-like switching behavior analogous to biological synapses — a pathway toward biological transistors for direct neural-digital data transfer.

Synthetic Protein Transistors as Biological Logic Gates: Toward Direct Neural-Digital Communication. Engineered proteins NPI-9 and NPI-11 demonstrate voltage-gated conformational switching in physiological buffer and produce reproducible current signatures time-locked to action potential firing when integrated with primary cortical neuron cultures — the first purely synthetic protein mediating digital-analog signal transduction at the neuron-electrode interface.

Mapping the Mycelium: Graph-Theoretic Approaches to Bio-Organic Network Topology. Stress-induced NPI filament networks exhibit small-world topology with clustering coefficients exceeding random graph baselines. Connectivity can be tuned by stress duration, temperature gradient, and initial concentration, offering a design space for programmable bio-organic substrates for neuromorphic computing.

papers.title: Project Mycelium: Resilient Bio-Organic Computing via Stress-Induced Protein Self-Assembly
Synthetic Protein Transistors as Biological Logic Gates
Mapping the Mycelium: Graph-Theoretic Approaches to Bio-Organic Network Topology

nsf_grants.title: CAREER: Neural-Protein Interfacing for Next-Generation Brain-Computer Integration

pi_survey.research_priorities: Our lab investigates Neural-Protein Interfacing (NPI) — engineering synthetic proteins that act as biological transistors, enabling direct data transfer between organic neural tissue and digital hardware. Project Mycelium, our primary initiative, maps how these proteins self-assemble under high-stress conditions to form resilient bio-organic computing frameworks.
pi_survey.mentorship_style: I expect my students to know what they're doing. I have a relatively frequent check with each one of them, and I expect them to have an idea on what their issues are and how to mix that.
pi_survey.lab_expectations: First-author publications are required for graduation, High-impact publications are strongly encouraged
pi_survey.student_qualities: Strong independent problem-solving ability, Strong technical or methodological skills, Consistent and reliable work habits
pi_survey.student_support: Encourage them to seek guidance from senior lab members (e.g., postdocs)
pi_survey.success_traits: They work very hard and are very smart. They know what to do instead of asking me every single thing.
pi_survey.poor_fit_traits: Requires constant direction or supervision, Low tolerance for uncertainty or failure, Misalignment with expected work pace or commitment
pi_survey.daily_experience: Fast-paced and dynamic, Structured and predictable, Individual project-focused
pi_survey.lab_description: You work a lot and learn a lot.
pi_survey.interview_questions: Research experience and past projects, Problem-solving or critical thinking ability, Ability to work independently

student_survey_responses.overall_experience: You work a hell lot, not only on your projects, but helping the PI do his chores etc. Again, really intense.
student_survey_responses.mentorship: He does not provide any "mentorship," but he WILL check your work very very very frequenctly. — meets Rarely
student_survey_responses.publication_rate: High — PI maintains an intense pace with strong publication expectations
student_survey_responses.when_struggling: You rely on postdocs/senior students, And the PI will show that they think you're stupid.
student_survey_responses.communication: Very direct (clear, blunt feedback), They're so MEAN.
student_survey_responses.comfort_with_feedback: Very difficult, also if you did go talk to them, it's only gonna make things worse.
student_survey_responses.success_type: Highly independent, Technically strong from the start, Really hard worker
student_survey_responses.struggle_type: Needs frequent direction, Work pace mismatch, Weak technical background
student_survey_responses.common_mismatch: Mentorship style, Workload expectations, Communication style
student_survey_responses.main_difference: They're kinda insane about wanting people to compete. However, our postdoc is actually pretty nice and helpful.
student_survey_responses.wish_knew: The PI is really really intense. This is a well paid position, however...Seriously consider your work life balance.
